answer:
\boxed{Mark Twain}

Lionel Trilling’s *"Reality in America"* (1950) critiques the American liberal tradition’s tendency to idealize Mark Twain’s work while dismissing Henry James as overly "European" or "refined." Twain’s satirical, populist style aligned with liberal democratic values, whereas James’s psychological complexity was seen as elitist or impractical. This contrast highlights Trilling’s broader argument about American cultural and intellectual priorities.
